title: Configure a resilient replica set
description: Use an odd number of voting members across failure domains so elections reach a clean majority.
body: Use when standing up or reviewing a replica set for high availability. Actions: deploy an odd number of voting members (three or five) spread across separate availability zones, add an arbiter only when an even count is otherwise unavoidable, set priorities so a preferred primary leads, and test a failover with rs.stepDown(). Verify the set re-elects a primary within the election timeout and writes resume. Validation signals include a new primary elected after stepDown and majority writes continuing without data loss. Risk boundary: placing a majority of voters in one zone means a zone outage halts writes, so confirm voter placement spans failure domains before declaring the set highly available.
